content.problemStatement:
Chef is a painter. He likes to draw geometric figures and among them, he especially loves isosceles right triangles. The longest side of a right triangle is called its hypotenuse. Chef drew NNN isosceles right triangles (numbered 111 through NNN) on an infinite sheet of paper, which can be represented by a Cartesian half-plane (containing all points (x,y)(x, y)(x,y) such that y≥0y \ge 0y≥0), in such a way that the hypotenuse of each triangle lies on the xxx-axis (the line y=0y=0y=0). For example, the resulting figure could look like this:

The sides of the triangles and the xxx-axis split the paper into one or more contiguous regions, which Chef calls districts. Chef is fascinated by his drawing, so he would like to know how many districts are on his paper. For each valid iii, the hypotenuse of the iii-th triangle is the line segment between points (li,0)(l_i, 0)(li​,0) and (ri,0)(r_i, 0)(ri​,0); note that the triangles are uniquely defined by this information.

Help Chef calculate the number of districts in the painting.
constraints: −109≤li<ri≤109-10^9 \le l_i \lt r_i \le 10^9−109≤li​<ri​≤109 for each valid iii
the pairs (l1,r1),(l2,r2),…,(lN,rN)(l_1, r_1), (l_2, r_2), \ldots, (l_N, r_N)(l1​,r1​),(l2​,r2​),…,(lN​,rN​) are distinct
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers lil_ili​ and rir_iri​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the number of districts.
